root_text.an10.74:1.1: “Dasahi, bhikkhave, vaḍḍhīhi vaḍḍhamāno ariyasāvako ariyāya vaḍḍhiyā vaḍḍhati, sārādāyī ca hoti varādāyī kāyassa.
root_text.an10.74:1.3: Khettavatthūhi vaḍḍhati, dhanadhaññena vaḍḍhati, puttadārehi vaḍḍhati, dāsakammakaraporisehi vaḍḍhati, catuppadehi vaḍḍhati, saddhāya vaḍḍhati, sīlena vaḍḍhati, sutena vaḍḍhati, cāgena vaḍḍhati, paññāya vaḍḍhati—
root_text.an10.74:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi vaḍḍhīhi vaḍḍhamāno ariyasāvako ariyāya vaḍḍhiyā vaḍḍhati, sārādāyī ca hoti varādāyī kāyassāti.

translation_text.an10.74:1.1: “Mendicants, a noble disciple who grows in ten ways grows nobly, taking on what is essential and excellent in this life.
translation_text.an10.74:1.3: He grows in fields and lands, money and grain, wives and children, in bondservants, workers, and staff, and in livestock. And he grows in faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom.
translation_text.an10.74:1.4: A noble disciple who grows in ten ways grows nobly, taking on what is essential and excellent in this life.
translation_text.an10.74:2.1: Someone who grows in money and grain,
translation_text.an10.74:2.4: by relatives and friends, and even by royals.
translation_text.an10.74:3.1: When someone grows in faith and ethics,
translation_text.an10.74:3.2: wisdom, and both generosity and learning—
translation_text.an10.74:3.3: a good man such as he sees clearly,
translation_text.an10.74:3.4: and in this very life he grows in both ways.”
